question_text: Two electric heaters are switched on simultaneously. Heater A has a power rating of 1500 W and Heater B has a power rating of 2500 W. Which heater transfers energy more rapidly, and why?
correct_answer: Heater B transfers energy more rapidly because it has a higher power rating of 2500 W compared to Heater A's 1500 W. Power is the rate of energy transfer, so the higher the power, the faster the energy is transferred.
explanation: Power tells us how quickly energy is being transferred. Think of it like pouring water: a wider tap (higher power) pours water faster than a narrower tap (lower power). Heater B with 2500 W transfers energy 1000 W faster than Heater A, meaning it delivers more energy every second.
